nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Pol
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Guillermo, Manuel, Jose, Juan, Rafael, Abelardo, Adolfo, Ana, Angel, Beatriz, Fernando, Gilberto.
Catalan and French (mainly northern, also Occitan): from the personal name Pol, equivalent to Paul .
Dutch and North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a grassy mound, from Middle Dutch pol ‘tussock’.

variants.name: Polus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois.
Polish (Poluś): from a pet form of the personal name Pol .
French: from a Latinized form of   Paul  .

variants.name: Polek
meanings: Polish: from a pet form of the personal name Pol (see Pol 5).

variants.name: Poll
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Heinz, Gerda, Gerhard, Kurt, Otto.
Dutch: variant of   Pol   2.
English (Norfolk): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Late Latin personal name, which was borne by a saint martyred in Rome, together with Saints Faustinus and Simplicius, in the early 4th century. The original form of the name was probably Viatrix, a feminine version of Viator ‘voyager (through life)’, which was a favourite name among early Christians. This was then altered by association with Latin Beatus ‘blessed’ (Via- and Bea- sometimes being pronounced the same in Late Latin). See also Beatrice .

en.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Saint Pol was born in France as the daughter of Hugues III dit Candavène , Comte de St. Pol, Comte de Hesdin and Beatrix de St.Pol. She married Robert I de Coucy. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in 1192.

fr.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Saint Pol est née à France, fille de Hugues III dit Candavène , Comte de St. Pol, Comte de Hesdin et Beatrix de St.Pol. Elle a épousé Robert I de Coucy. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée en 1192.

de.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Saint Pol wurde in Frankreich, als Tochter von Hugues III dit Candavène , Comte de St. Pol, Comte de Hesdin und Beatrix de St.Pol geboren. Sie hat Robert I de Coucy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb 1192.

it.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Saint Pol è nata il nel Francia come figlia di Hugues III dit Candavène , Comte de St. Pol, Comte de Hesdin e Beatrix de St.Pol. Lei sposato Robert I de Coucy. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1192.

ja.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Saint PolはフランスでHugues III dit Candavène , Comte de St. Pol, Comte de HesdinとBeatrix de St.Polの娘として生まれた。Robert I de Coucyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1192年に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Saint Pol(은)는 프랑스에서 Hugues III dit Candavène , Comte de St. Pol, Comte de Hesdin(와)과 Beatrix de St.Pol의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Robert I de Coucy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1192년 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Saint Pol nasceu em França filha de Hugues III dit Candavène , Comte de St. Pol, Comte de Hesdin e Beatrix de St.Pol. Ela casou-se com Robert I de Coucy. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1192.

ru.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Saint Pol родилась в местности Франция. Её родителей звали Hugues III dit Candavène , Comte de St. Pol, Comte de Hesdin и Beatrix de St.Pol. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert I de Coucy. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла в 1192 г.

es.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Saint Pol nació en Francia como hija de Hugues III dit Candavène , Comte de St. Pol, Comte de Hesdin y Beatrix de St.Pol. Se casó con Robert I de Coucy. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1192.
